rationale: The cited paper appears only as a single benchmark reference when the authors note that their observed intransitivity rate is a notable level in prior literature. The citing paper's core theory and mechanism are built around the compatibility principle (e.g., Tversky, Slovic, and related work), not around the cited paper's incomplete-information account. There is no clear use of the cited paper for hypothesis development, design borrowing, or interpretation beyond this passing contextual comparison.
